scope_contract: cleanliness-of-this-diff only; pre-existing bulk in pre-existing files is OUT OF SCOPE per Section 5.4 rule 3

new_vs_pre_existing_mechanism.description: Each finding (when emitted) carries an explicit introduced_in_diff boolean. Per AC-12.1, close.md uses this tag as the NEW-positive marker; absent/null/untagged findings are treated as pre-existing/advisory/non-blocking by default-safe rule.
new_vs_pre_existing_mechanism.determination_method: Verified via git diff HEAD against the 6 in-scope .md files. test-writer.md is wholly NEW (entire file is introduced_in_diff=true content). The other 5 files received small additive diffs (+22/+29/+58/+75/+30 lines respectively); their pre-existing bulk (1429+/943+/1701+/1403+/383+ lines) is out of scope.

summary.verdict: PASS — zero verbosity violations introduced in this diff

rationale.rules_not_stories_compliance: All 212 lines of additive content across the 5 modified files plus the 221 lines of the new test-writer.md are procedural: shell commands, conditional logic, JSON schema fragments, gate evaluation rules, attribution enums, and dispatch templates. No Philosophy/Overview/Quality-Standards/Safety-Features narrative sections were introduced.
rationale.inline_doc_fields_assessment: The inline `_risk_level_doc`, `_primary_cause_doc`, and `_doc` strings inside the JSON schema examples (agents/ba.md:1127, agents/qa.md:1392/1396/1405) are load-bearing schema annotations — they encode the authoritative enum semantics consumed by score-update.sh attribution and the gate-evaluation logic in commands/dev.md Step 8. These are rules-as-data, not stories.
rationale.test_writer_md_assessment: The new 221-line agents/test-writer.md has zero superfluous content. Every section earns its keep: Authority Chain (5 lines = scope statement), Input Contract (8 lines = required fields), Output Contract (90 lines = the two artifact families with literal schemas, which are the agent's deliverable contract), UPDATE-vs-CREATE Logic (12 lines = the 6 keying rules), hook_check Auto-Derivation (12 lines = type-keyed required keys), Pytest.fail Hardblock Pattern (7 lines = the sentinel contract), Procedure (10 lines = 6 numbered steps), Output JSON Report (16 lines = report schema), Quality Checklist (8 lines = 7 acceptance checks). There is no Philosophy section, no Overview section, no narrative explanation of why pytest matters — only what to produce and how to key it.
rationale.code_block_assessment: Code/JSON blocks (test-writer skeleton template, manifest schema, dev-report blast_radius_declarations[] example, score-update Mascot Score Changes table) are all load-bearing schema/template specimens — the readers (Dev, QA, downstream agents) consume them as literal contracts. None exceed the 30-line-per-template warning threshold from this inspector's catalog.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh reasoning) confirmed the draft ZERO-findings verdict. Verbatim: 'CODEX_FEEDBACK: no issues found.' Codex explicitly verified (a) no new Philosophy/Overview/Quality-Standards/Safety-Features/tutorial content in the diff, (b) the _doc / _risk_level_doc / _primary_cause_doc fields are concise procedural schema annotations rather than verbose narrative, (c) the 221 lines of agents/test-writer.md are justified by contracts/schemas/templates/update-logic/hardblock-pattern/procedure/checklist with no superfluous story section, (d) nothing in the diff reaches even the minor/advisory verbosity threshold. Codex also noted one OBSERVATION_ONLY: the mascot-score placement text is repeated across BA/Dev/QA dispatches in commands/dev.md, but classified that as style preference (dispatch-local and load-bearing), not an audit finding.

findings.issue: Mascot-score placement text repeats across BA/Dev/QA dispatch blocks in commands/dev.md (3 near-identical 2-3-line pre-dispatch blocks).
findings.rationale: Codex explicitly classified as 'style preference, not an audit finding' because each block is dispatch-local and load-bearing. The repetition is intentional — each dispatch site needs its own injection rule literally adjacent to the Task-tool invocation. Per the codex-consultation contract, OBSERVATION_ONLY findings without a PROPOSED_FIX are logged and do not block the cycle.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: No findings to incorporate — codex confirmed the ZERO-findings draft. The single OBSERVATION_ONLY is logged for future-cycle awareness but does not change this report's verdict.
